summary: Golden Road shines in a tight four‑song early Summer‑of‑Love set
blurb: The four‑song set features a vibrant Golden Road and swirling Pigpen organ. Jerry’s early Gibson guitar work is free and brilliant, while Phil lays down hot bass lines on Alligator and New Potato. Soundboard captures are crystal‑clear, making this an essential early‑'67 performance.
review: May 5 1967 at the Fillmore captured a brief but electrifying four‑song set that foreshadowed the Summer of Love vibe the Dead would soon unleash. Opening with “He Was A Friend Of Mine,” Jerry Garcia already displays his nascent Gibson tone, delivering a solo described as "brilliant" and full of freedom. The band rolls seamlessly into “The Golden Road [To Unlimited Devotion],” which reviewers repeatedly call beautiful and a highlight of the night, while Pigpen’s organ swirls with that classic 1967 psychedelic whizz. Phil Lesh’s bass burns hot on both “Alligator” and “New Potato Caboose,” driving the group forward as Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming locks in solidly. Though an audience tape exists, the soundboard versions – especially gd1967-05-05.126250.sbd.wolfe‑smith.flac16 – deliver crystal‑clear balance, letting every instrument shine and preserving the historic energy of this rare early set.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are marked positive; reviewers consistently praise Jerry’s early Gibson tone, Pigpen’s swirling organ, Phil’s hot bass lines, and the standout songs (Golden Road, New Potato Caboose, Alligator, He Was A Friend Of Mine). No negative comments about performance appear, indicating strong positive sentiment across multiple sources.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crystal‑clear, well‑balanced audio that showcases the band's playing far better than the fair‑quality audience tape.

key_highlights: Golden Road shines as a beautiful early‑Gibson showcase.
Pigpen’s organ provides a whizzing, psychedelic backdrop throughout the set.
Phil Lesh delivers hot, on‑fire bass lines on Alligator and New Potato Caboose.
The soundboard recordings are praised as the best‑sounding versions of this rare 1967 show.
song_highlights: He Was A Friend Of Mine

band_performance.Jerry: Shows early Gibson tone; guitar work described as brilliant, free and excellent throughout the set.
band_performance.Phil: Bass lines are hot and on fire, especially during Alligator and New Potato Caboose.
band_performance.Billy: Drumming is solid and drives the performance; percussion noted as a strong foundation despite some uncertainty about drummer identity.

must_listen_sequences: He Was A Friend Of Mine
